description: All central and state government schemes for Indian farmers, structured for semantic search in ChromaDB

documents.document: PM-KISAN Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi provides income support of 6000 rupees per year in three equal instalments to all landholding farmer families. Applicable to all landholding farmer families irrespective of the size of landholdings. Apply at pmkisan.gov.in

Kisan Credit Card KCC allows farmers to avail short-term loans without stringent credit checks at affordable interest rates to acquire seeds, fertilizers, and farming machinery. Apply via nationalised banks, co-operative banks, or rural banks.

metadata.apply_url: Nationalised banks, co-operative banks, rural banks

documents.document: Modified Interest Subvention Scheme MISS provides short-term agricultural loans up to 3 lakh rupees at an annual interest rate of 7 percent. An additional 3 percent subvention is provided for prompt and timely repayment, effectively making the rate 4 percent for punctual borrowers.

metadata.benefit_amount: Loans up to ₹3 lakh at 7% (4% for timely repayment)
metadata.eligibility: All farmers taking short-term agricultural loans

documents.document: PMFBY Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana provides crop insurance against natural calamities, pests, and diseases. Premium rates are 2 percent for Kharif crops, 1.5 percent for Rabi crops, and 5 percent for horticultural crops. Apply at pmfby.gov.in

metadata.benefit_amount: Full coverage against losses; premium: 2% Kharif, 1.5% Rabi, 5% Horticulture

documents.document: Restructured Weather Based Crop Insurance Scheme RWBCIS launched alongside PMFBY in 2016 to protect farmers from losses due to adverse weather conditions including rainfall, temperature, wind, and humidity.

metadata.scheme_name: Restructured Weather Based Crop Insurance Scheme (RWBCIS)

documents.document: MSP Minimum Support Price system ensures fair prices for farmers. MSP is recommended by CACP and declared for 23 crops, protecting farmers from distress sales in the open market.

metadata.benefit_amount: Declared per crop per quintal (e.g., Wheat ₹2,425/quintal, Paddy ₹2,300/quintal)
metadata.eligibility: All farmers growing the 23 notified crops

documents.document: PM Krishi Sinchayee Yojana PMKSY Har Khet Ko Pani focuses on improving water use efficiency through micro-irrigation including drip and sprinkler systems with the goal of ensuring every field gets water.

metadata.scheme_name: PM Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY) — Har Khet Ko Pani

documents.document: Per Drop More Crop PDMC launched in 2015-16 offers financial assistance of 55 percent to small and marginal farmers and 45 percent to others for micro-irrigation installation through drip and sprinkler systems.

metadata.benefit_amount: 55% subsidy for small/marginal farmers; 45% for others
metadata.eligibility: All farmers; higher subsidy for small and marginal farmers

documents.document: PM-KUSUM Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan subsidises farmers to install solar irrigation pumps. Each farmer receives a 60 percent subsidy to set up tube wells and pump sets. The scheme aims to increase solar capacity up to 34800 MW by March 2026.

metadata.scheme_name: PM-KUSUM (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan)

documents.document: Soil Health Card SHC Scheme assists state governments in issuing Soil Health Cards to farmers with detailed soil testing reports. As of 2024-25, 75 lakh soil samples collected with 1000 soil testing labs set up across India. Apply at soilhealth.dac.gov.in

Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana PKVY promotes organic farming clusters, reducing dependency on chemicals. Enhances soil health and boosts organic produce markets through cluster-based implementation.

National Mission on Natural Farming NMNF is a standalone Centrally Sponsored Scheme with a total outlay of 2481 crore rupees to promote natural farming in mission mode across the country providing training support and financial aid to farmers who adopt natural farming practices.

metadata.benefit_type: Financial Aid + Training for Natural Farming
metadata.eligibility: All farmers willing to adopt natural farming

documents.document: e-NAM National Agriculture Market integrates APMCs across India into a single digital market platform. Promotes competition and helps farmers get better prices by enabling them to sell produce online reducing dependence on middlemen. Apply at enam.gov.in

RKVY-RAFTAAR Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana empowers states to plan agriculture strategies based on local needs funding agricultural innovation infrastructure development and startups in agri-business.

Agriculture Infrastructure Fund AIF launched with an allocation of 1 lakh crore rupees provides medium to long-term debt financing for post-harvest infrastructure. Offers interest subvention of 3 percent per annum on loans up to 2 crore rupees for up to 7 years. Since inception in 2020, AIF has sanctioned 51364 crore rupees for 84159 projects including warehouses cold storage and processing centres.

metadata.benefit_amount: 3% interest subvention on loans up to ₹2 crore for 7 years

documents.document: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanisation SMAM accelerates spread of agricultural mechanisation in India. Provides a subsidy of up to 50 percent on farm equipment especially for small and marginal farmers SC ST farmers and those in North-East regions. Apply at agrimachinery.nic.in

metadata.benefit_amount: Up to 50% subsidy on farm equipment
metadata.eligibility: All farmers; higher for small/marginal, SC/ST, North-East farmers

documents.document: Namo Drone Didi Scheme is a 1261 crore rupee scheme to provide drones to 15000 Women Self Help Groups for offering rental services to farmers for applying fertilisers and pesticides. SHGs receive an 80 percent subsidy up to 8 lakh rupees and earn at least 1 lakh rupees annually from drone rental income.

metadata.benefit_type: Drone Subsidy for SHGs + Rental Income
metadata.benefit_amount: 80% subsidy up to ₹8 lakh; ₹1 lakh+ annual income from drone rentals

documents.document: Digital Agriculture Mission DAM approved by the Union Cabinet in September 2024 with an outlay of 2817 crore rupees. An umbrella scheme creating Digital Public Infrastructure through two pillars Agri Stack and Krishi Decision Support System enabling farmer-centric digital services for timely and reliable information.

Mission for Integrated Development of Horticulture MIDH focuses on the overall growth of the horticulture sector boosting production improving nutritional security and augmenting farmer incomes through integrated support.

metadata.scheme_name: Mission for Integrated Development of Horticulture (MIDH)

documents.document: National Food Security Mission NFSM aims to expand the production of staples like rice wheat pulses and commercial crops while restoring soil fertility and increasing agricultural productivity sustainably.

metadata.eligibility: Farmers growing rice, wheat, pulses, commercial crops

documents.document: National Mission on Edible Oils Oil Palm NMEO-OP launched in 2021 provides incentives to farmers to grow oil palm trees and promotes the development of oil palm processing mills aiming to significantly reduce India's dependence on imported edible oils.

metadata.scheme_name: National Mission on Edible Oils – Oil Palm (NMEO-OP)
metadata.eligibility: Farmers willing to grow oil palm

documents.document: National Bamboo Mission NBM promotes holistic growth of the bamboo sector through area-based regionally differentiated strategy increasing area under bamboo plantation in non-forest government and private lands for additional farmer income.

National Beekeeping and Honey Mission NBHM launched in 2020 promotes scientific beekeeping and honey production. Provides training equipment and financial assistance to beekeepers. Honey production has increased from 12000 metric tons in 2019-20 to 20000 metric tons in 2022-23.

metadata.benefit_type: Training + Equipment + Financial Aid for Beekeeping

documents.document: Mukhyamantri Krishak Durghatna Kalyan Yojana Uttar Pradesh protects farmers and their families from financial hardships caused by accidents during agricultural work. Launched September 14 2019 it provides assistance up to 5 lakh rupees in case of death or complete loss of both hands feet or eyes. Has benefited more than 63000 farmers.

metadata.benefit_amount: Up to ₹5 lakh (death or permanent disability)
metadata.eligibility: Farmers and families affected by agricultural accidents

documents.document: Mukhyamantri Khet Surakhsha Yojana Uttar Pradesh protects farmers crops from damage caused by wild animals. The government provides a grant of 1.43 lakh rupees to install solar fencing using a 12 volt current system covering 60 percent of the cost per hectare. 50 crore rupees has been allocated for implementation.

metadata.benefit_amount: ₹1.43 lakh grant; 60% of cost per hectare
metadata.eligibility: Farmers facing crop damage from wild animals

documents.document: Mukhyamantri Krishak Kalyankari Yojana Uttar Pradesh provides financial assistance through the Mandi Parishad. Has extended financial assistance worth 134.76 crore rupees to 79796 farmers under this scheme.

Soil Health Card Scheme Uttar Pradesh in 2024-25 the government distributed 66 lakh quintals of high-quality seeds 95 lakh metric tonnes of fertilizers and issued 8.5 lakh soil health cards enabling farmers to enhance soil fertility and optimize crop yields.

metadata.benefit_amount: 66 lakh quintals seeds, 95 lakh MT fertilizers, 8.5 lakh soil cards (2024-25)

documents.document: Pest and Disease Control Scheme Krishi Raksha Yojana Uttar Pradesh provides grants including 75 percent subsidy maximum 500 rupees per hectare on bio-pesticides and bio-agents 75 percent subsidy maximum 150 rupees per hectare on seed treatment chemicals and 50 percent subsidy maximum 500 rupees per hectare on farm protection chemicals. All farmer categories including SC ST and women farmers are eligible.

metadata.scheme_name: Pest & Disease Control Scheme (Krishi Raksha Yojana)
metadata.benefit_type: Subsidy on Pesticides and Crop Protection
metadata.benefit_amount: 75% subsidy on bio-pesticides; 50% on farm protection chemicals
metadata.eligibility: All farmers including SC/ST and women

documents.document: Trained Agricultural Entrepreneur Swavalamban Agri Junction Scheme Uttar Pradesh has established 6608 agri-junctions functioning as one-stop service centres providing farmers with better access to inputs and services.

metadata.scheme_name: Trained Agricultural Entrepreneur Swavalamban Agri Junction Scheme
metadata.eligibility: Trained agricultural entrepreneurs, farmers seeking inputs and services

documents.document: Drone Subsidy Scheme Uttar Pradesh the state introduced drones in agriculture offering a 40 to 50 percent subsidy to Farmer Producer Organizations and agricultural graduates.

UP Agriculture Export Policy approved in September 2019 aims to double agricultural exports from the state increase farmer income assess international markets and provide information on exportable products and global opportunities to farmers.

metadata.eligibility: All UP farmers with exportable produce

documents.document: e-Mandi and Digital Mandi Reforms Uttar Pradesh by January 2025 digital transactions worth 6999 crore rupees had been completed across 125 mandis. As part of the e-Mandi initiative 922 e-licenses were issued and over 4.18 crore online slips were generated.

metadata.eligibility: All UP farmers selling produce at mandis

documents.document: UP AGREES World Bank Project Uttar Pradesh launched in collaboration with the World Bank this landmark initiative benefits farmers in 21 Purvanchal and 7 districts of Bundelkhand.

metadata.eligibility: Farmers in 21 Purvanchal districts and 7 Bundelkhand districts

documents.document: MP Mukhyamantri Krishak Fasal Uparjan Sahayata Yojana Madhya Pradesh implemented to ensure farmers receive fair prices for their crops. Supports farmers by addressing operational losses in grain procurement ensuring farmers get the full Minimum Support Price of 5328 rupees per quintal.

metadata.scheme_name: MP Mukhyamantri Krishak Fasal Uparjan Sahayata Yojana

documents.document: Bhavantar Bhugtan Yojana Madhya Pradesh launched by the MP government compensates farmers when the market price of their produce goes below the MSP. The difference is deposited directly into the farmer's bank account.

metadata.eligibility: All MP farmers; activates when market price < MSP

documents.document: MP Kisan Kalyan Mission 2024-25 Madhya Pradesh announced on Independence Day 2024 by Chief Minister Mohan Yadav launched November 1 2024. Farmers get help with advanced farming techniques seeds fertilizers and equipment. Experts provide training to increase crop production. Also covers horticulture crop insurance loans and technical support.

metadata.benefit_type: Comprehensive Mission — Training + Inputs + Insurance + Loans

documents.document: Madhya Pradesh Organic Farming Mission MPOFM promotes organic farming practices among farmers advocating for sustainable agriculture and fostering better health outcomes for farmers and consumers.

metadata.eligibility: All MP farmers willing to adopt organic farming

documents.document: National Mission on Natural Farming implementation in Madhya Pradesh guides farmers towards Zero Budget Natural Farming using cow dung cow urine Jeevamrit Beejamrit and Ghanjeevamrit. Improves soil fertility reduces production costs and ensures naturally safe crops. Training camps organized in selected blocks of every district.

metadata.scheme_name: National Mission on Natural Farming (MP Implementation)

documents.document: Chief Minister Agriculture and Rural Development Scheme CMKGY Madhya Pradesh focused on rural areas and overall agricultural development offering financial support for agricultural mechanisation crop diversification and post-harvest activities.

metadata.scheme_name: Chief Minister Agriculture & Rural Development Scheme (CMKGY)

documents.document: Atmanirbhar MP Agriculture Infrastructure Fund Madhya Pradesh post-harvest projects such as warehouse construction processing units and e-mandi approved. Loan facility provided to cooperatives at 1 percent interest rate. 239 projects worth 57.28 crore rupees sanctioned by district banks.

metadata.benefit_amount: 1% interest loans for cooperatives; ₹57.28 crore for 239 projects

documents.document: Bhandar Grih Nirman Yojana Godown Construction Madhya Pradesh started to increase storage capacity in rural areas. Government land allotted free of cost to Primary Agricultural Credit Societies PACS for godown construction with 50 percent loan 20 percent NCDC subsidy and 30 percent state government subsidy.

metadata.benefit_amount: 50% loan + 20% NCDC subsidy + 30% state subsidy; free government land

documents.document: Ladli Behna Yojana Madhya Pradesh provides monthly financial assistance of 1250 rupees to eligible women aged 21 to 60. Encourages participation of women in family farming and allied activities improving decision-making power and investments in backyard farming poultry dairy and small-scale agricultural enterprises.

Crop Residue Management CRM Loan Scheme Punjab introduced by CM Bhagwant Mann through cooperative banks provides farmers with CRM machines with a 50 to 80 percent subsidy on costs. Since 2018 over 1.58 lakh CRM machine units provided to farmers with more than 16000 sanction letters issued in 2025. Stubble burning incidents declined 53 percent in Kharif season 2025.

Direct Seeding of Rice DSR Incentive Punjab supported by a 1500 rupees per acre incentive for water-conserving DSR technology. Adoption increased 17 percent with area under DSR rising from 2.53 lakh acres in 2024 to 2.96 lakh acres in 2025 marking a crucial milestone in groundwater preservation.

metadata.eligibility: Punjab paddy farmers adopting DSR technology

documents.document: Kharif Maize Diversification Pilot Punjab a pilot project in six districts Sangrur Bathinda Gurdaspur Jalandhar Kapurthala and Pathankot successfully transitioned over 11000 acres from paddy to Kharif maize supported by a 17500 rupees per hectare incentive plus 10000 rupees per hectare for inputs under RKVY.

metadata.eligibility: Farmers in Sangrur, Bathinda, Gurdaspur, Jalandhar, Kapurthala, Pathankot transitioning from paddy to maize

documents.document: BT Cotton Seed Subsidy Punjab cotton cultivation grew by 20 percent reaching 1.19 lakh hectares fuelled by a 33 percent government subsidy on BT cotton seeds recommended by Punjab Agricultural University.

KCC Punjab Special Implementation Punjab's 25 lakh KCC holders access 40000 to 50000 crore rupees annually at a subsidised 4 percent interest rate. PM-KISAN injects 1380 crore rupees yearly into 23 lakh farmers accounts. The 2025-26 Budget increased KCC loan limits from 3 lakh to 5 lakh rupees.

metadata.benefit_amount: Up to ₹5 lakh at 4% interest rate (2025-26)

documents.document: MSP Procurement Guarantee Wheat and Paddy Punjab MSP stands at 2425 rupees per quintal for wheat and 2300 rupees for paddy for 2024-25. The Food Corporation of India procures 92 percent of Punjab's rice and 72 percent of its wheat at MSP ensuring farmer income stability.

metadata.eligibility: All Punjab wheat and paddy farmers

documents.document: PMFME Scheme Punjab Agro total outlay of 306 crore rupees over six years 2020-21 to 2025-26 shared 60 to 40 between central and state governments. Target is to provide financial assistance to 7373 food processing units. Individual subsidy 35 percent of eligible project cost maximum 10 lakh rupees.

metadata.benefit_amount: 35% subsidy up to ₹10 lakh per unit
metadata.eligibility: Food processing entrepreneurs and farmer-entrepreneurs in Punjab

documents.document: Maharashtra AI Agriculture Policy Maharashtra introduced India's first dedicated AI Agriculture Policy with a 500 crore rupee budget to bring Artificial Intelligence and automation to farming. Support from 4 state agriculture universities and startup hubs. Target to benefit 50000 plus farmers and 1 lakh acres of land.

Agristack Agricultural Digital Public Infrastructure Maharashtra a new initiative to ensure efficient digital service delivery of government schemes to farmers creating digital identity databases covering land details crop history subsidy access and insurance records.

MahaDBT Farmer Scheme Maharashtra state government provides subsidies to all farmers to purchase heavy machinery. General category farmers receive 40 percent subsidy and SC ST farmers receive 50 percent subsidy. Applications submitted online at home without visiting a government office.

metadata.eligibility: All Maharashtra farmers; higher subsidy for SC/ST

documents.document: State Agriculture Mechanisation Scheme Maharashtra provides financial assistance for tractors power tillers drip irrigation sprinkler systems greenhouses and other farm machinery through the MahaDBT portal with first-come-first-served priority from 2025-26.

Mukhyamantri Solar Krishi Vahini Yojana Maharashtra aims to become the first state to make all agricultural power lines solar-powered. In nine months 119 power lines with 147 MW cumulative solar capacity operationalised. Over 1 lakh solar water pumps being installed.

metadata.benefit_amount: 1 lakh solar water pumps being installed

documents.document: Bhavantar Yojana Maharashtra launched to guarantee minimum support prices for crops protecting farmers from severe market price fluctuations. Ensures farmers receive fair prices regardless of market conditions. Uses satellite-based crop monitoring and digital payment systems for implementation.

metadata.eligibility: All Maharashtra farmers selling notified crops

documents.document: One Taluka One Agricultural Market Committee Scheme Maharashtra being implemented to ensure fair agricultural trade with over 700000 metric tonnes of paddy and 171 metric tonnes of coarse grains procured in the 2024-25 Kharif marketing season.

metadata.scheme_name: One Taluka One Agricultural Market Committee Scheme
metadata.eligibility: Farmers in every taluka of Maharashtra

documents.document: Natural Calamity Relief Maharashtra the government swiftly provided relief to farmers affected by natural calamities distributing 814 crore rupees to more than 7.8 lakh farmers in 2024-25.

metadata.eligibility: Maharashtra farmers affected by natural calamities

documents.document: Dr Babasaheb Ambedkar Krishi Swalamban Yojana Maharashtra provides financial assistance specifically to SC farmers for irrigation infrastructure including wells pumps and drip systems helping the most marginalised farming communities.

metadata.scheme_name: Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Krishi Swalamban Yojana

documents.document: Honey Cafe and Beekeeping Scheme Maharashtra establishes Honey Cafes in tourist locations like Mahabaleshwar and Lonavala. Government purchase rate is 500 rupees per kg for honey and 300 rupees per kg for wax. Helps farmers diversify income.

Krishak Bandhu Natun Scheme West Bengal flagship scheme for farmers with 1 acre or more of land who get 10000 rupees every year split into two payments for Kharif June and Rabi November seasons. Those with less than 1 acre receive money on a pro-rata basis with a minimum of 4000 rupees per year. Since inception in 2019 the scheme has disbursed a total of 24086 crore rupees. For Kharif 2025 2930 crore rupees is being transferred to about 1.09 crore farmers.

metadata.eligibility: All registered farmers in West Bengal

documents.document: Krishak Bandhu Death Benefit Scheme West Bengal provides one-time financial grant of 2 lakh rupees to the next of kin in case of untimely demise of a registered farmer between ages 18 to 60. Insurance payout processed within 15 days of the farmer's death. Covers even death by suicide.

metadata.eligibility: Next of kin of registered WB farmers aged 18-60

documents.document: Krishak Bandhu Smart Card West Bengal the government provides a digitised Krishak Bandhu Smart Card to every eligible farmer serving as their single digital identity for all state government schemes. Helps with direct transfer of all scheme benefits to the farmer's bank account ensuring full transparency.

metadata.benefit_type: Digital Identity Card for Scheme Access

documents.document: State Paddy Procurement Preference West Bengal farmers registered in the Krishak Bandhu scheme get preference in the State Government's paddy procurement scheme implemented by the Food and Supplies Department.
Lakshmir Bhandar Scheme West Bengal a woman member of a farmer's family can apply for this scheme and receive monthly financial assistance providing supplementary income to farming households.

metadata.eligibility: Women members of farming families in West Bengal

schema_notes.id_format: <state_code>_<serial_number> (central = 'central', up = Uttar Pradesh, mp = Madhya Pradesh, pb = Punjab, mh = Maharashtra, wb = West Bengal)

chromadb_usage.recommended_embedding_model: text-embedding-3-small or any multilingual model for Hindi keywords

suggested_filters_for_portal: state (Central / UP / MP / Punjab / Maharashtra / West Bengal)
category (Income Support, Insurance, Irrigation, Soil, Market, Infrastructure, Technology, Specialised Crops)
benefit_type (Cash Transfer, Loan, Subsidy, Insurance, Training, Market Access)
